methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The tracked fund balance must never exceed the actual ETH balance of the NukeFund contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the tracked fund exceeds the actual balance, users attempting to nuke their NFTs will experience reverts due to insufficient ETH, leading to a denial of service and loss of earned yield.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_nukeFundSolvency() public view { assert(fund <= address(this).balance); }

invariants.description: The number of tokens minted in any generation must not exceed the maximum allowed tokens per generation.
invariants.attack_scenario: Bypassing this limit allows an attacker to infinitely mint tokens in a specific generation, inflating the NFT supply, diluting the NukeFund share per token, and manipulating the generation-based price curve.

invariants.description: An entity's forging count must never exceed its forge potential within a reset period.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can forge more times than their entity's potential allows, they can over-breed high-value entities, inflating the supply of rare traits and extracting excess forging fees from the market.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_forgePotential(uint256 tokenId) public view { uint8 potential = uint8((nftContract.getTokenEntropy(tokenId) / 10) % 10); assert(forgingCounts[tokenId] <= potential || potential == 0); }

invariants.description: The totalDevWeight must exactly equal the sum of all individual developer weights.
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalDevWeight is less than the sum of individual weights, the contract will distribute more rewards than it receives, leading to insolvency. If it is greater, ETH will be permanently trapped in the contract.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalDevWeight() public view { uint256 sum = 0; /* iterate users to calculate sum */ assert(totalDevWeight == sum); }

invariants.description: If an NFT is actively listed for sale, the EntityTrading contract must be the owner of the NFT.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can list an NFT but retain ownership, they could double-spend the NFT by nuking it for ETH while simultaneously selling it to a buyer, extracting double value from the protocol.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tradingCustody(uint256 listingId) public view { if(listings[listingId].isActive) { assert(nftContract.ownerOf(listings[listingId].tokenId) == address(this)); } }

invariants.description: The sum of the developer fee and the forger's share must exactly equal the forging fee paid.
invariants.attack_scenario: Rounding errors or logic flaws in the fee split calculation could result in the sum being less than the fee paid (trapping ETH) or greater than the fee paid (draining the contract).
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_feeSplit(uint256 forgingFee) public view { uint256 devFee = forgingFee / taxCut; uint256 forgerShare = forgingFee - devFee; assert(devFee + forgerShare == forgingFee); }
